frameworks.name: continuous-time recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: dynamical-systems model of biological visual circuits with recurrent dynamics

frameworks.name: convolutional neural network
frameworks.one_line: CNN spatial processing combined with RNN continuous-time dynamics in hybrid architecture

frameworks.name: multi-area neural network
frameworks.one_line: stacked RNN areas trained end-to-end on cognitive tasks

core_question: Can a hybrid RNN-CNN architecture preserve biological recurrent dynamics while matching CNN visual performance?
key_assumptions: continuous-time recurrent dynamics better reflect biological circuit dynamics than feedforward CNNs
spatial convolution remains useful for efficient visual feature processing
manipulations: architecture: hybrid RNN-CNN vs conventional CNN
input noise level on visual benchmarks
single-area vs multi-area RNN front-end for cognitive tasks
key_findings: hybrid models match conventional CNN performance on ImageNet
hybrid models are more robust to input noise than conventional CNNs
multi-area RNNs perform complex cognitive tasks with the hybrid front-end
models capture time-dependent neural activity variations in higher-order visual areas of monkeys
interpretations: recurrent dynamics provide intrinsic noise-suppression while retaining vision-task performance
hybrid architectures bridge biological realism and large-scale visual processing
